extraction.one_line_summary: MERLIER Tim won the coastal sprint on Stage 6, perfectly timing his acceleration to hold off ACKERMAN and CORT after GAVIRIA launched first.
tactical_patterns: Gaviria launching first from distance failed as Merlier held position and timed late acceleration better under 200m.
Lot Soudal paced aggressively under 1km to delay Hagen's catch, maximizing teammate breakaway exposure despite inevitable failure.
Israel Start-Up Nation controlled final kilometer more than Deceuninck-QuickStep despite having two white-blue riders in position.

rider_signals.MERLIER Tim: positive: Recovered from Stage 1 crash to win perfectly-timed sprint, first WorldTour victory suggests adaptation to higher level
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: Launching sprint from too far out allowed Merlier to time pass perfectly in final meters
rider_signals.HAGEN Carl Frederick: neutral: 160km solo breakaway effort after companions dropped shows professional commitment but tactically futile
